91	def score_query_document_relevance(
92	    query: str,
93	    documents: Sequence[str],
94	    *,
95	    model_id: str,
96	    device: Optional[str] = None,
97	) -> list[float]:
98	    """Score each document's relevance to ``query`` as P("yes") in [0, 1], in input order.
99	
100	    Loads ``model_id`` as an ``AutoModelForCausalLM`` (the CORRECT class for a Qwen3 reranker)
101	    and reads the next-token "yes"/"no" logits — the canonical, bake-off-proven method. A real
102	    relevance signal: a clearly-relevant document scores markedly HIGHER than junk (unlike the
103	    ~0.5 noise the random CrossEncoder head produced).
104	
105	    Raises on any load/scoring failure (the caller falls back loudly to the original order).
106	    torch + transformers are imported here, lazily, so the reranker-OFF path never touches them.
107	    """
108	    docs = list(documents)
109	    if not docs:
110	        return []
111	
112	    # I-deepfix-001 P1-1 (#1344): the STRUCTURAL LOAD region. A failure HERE (import /
113	    # from_pretrained / device placement / token-id resolution) is a structural W7-dark
114	    # signal -> re-raise as RerankerLoadError so the caller marks the gate dark. A
115	    # later FORWARD-PASS failure (below, in _score_one) is a transient and is NOT
116	    # wrapped, so it does not flip the sticky structural signal.
117	    try:
118	        import torch  # lazy — nothing here loads on the reranker-OFF path
119	        from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer
120	
121	        resolved_device = _resolve_device(device)
122	        logger.info(
123	            "[qwen-reranker] loading %s on %s (causal-LM yes/no-logit scoring; NOT CrossEncoder)",
124	            model_id, resolved_device,
125	        )
126	        tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id, padding_side="left")
127	        model = (
128	            AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_id, dtype="auto")
129	            .to(resolved_device)
130	            .eval()
131	        )
132	        token_id_no = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids("no")
133	        token_id_yes = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids("yes")
134	    except Exception as load_exc:  # structural import / load / device / token failure
135	        raise RerankerLoadError(
136	            f"W7 reranker structural load failed for {model_id!r}: {load_exc}"
137	        ) from load_exc
138	
139	    def _score_one(document: str) -> float:
140	        text = (
141	            _SYSTEM_PREFIX
142	            + f"<Instruct>: {_INSTRUCT}\n<Query>: {query or ''}\n<Document>: {document}"
143	            + _ASSISTANT_SUFFIX
144	        )
145	        inputs = tokenizer(
146	            text,
147	            return_tensors="pt",
148	            truncation=True,
149	            max_length=_MAX_INPUT_TOKENS,

195	        sel = os.getenv("PG_EMBEDDER_MODEL", "").strip().lower()

214	        Qwen/Qwen3-Reranker-4B — the recency-completion pick (I-recency-001 #1296: wins BOTH axes,

12846	        # actually fired in THIS process. On a --resume the corpus is RECONSTRUCTED from a

12849	        # is necessarily None on the rebuilt object. Checking it false-aborts EVERY resume

3212	def build_d8_unadjudicated_banner(release_disclosure: "dict | None") -> str:
3213	    """B08 (#1352) finding #1: a run-specific top-of-report banner surfaced into report.md
3214	    when the strongest verifier (four-role D8) did NOT adjudicate this run.
3215	
3216	    The A18 always-release path serializes the honest ``adjudicated`` flag into
3217	    ``manifest['release_disclosure']`` — but a reader of report.md ALONE could not tell the
3218	    keystone verifier was skipped, because that disclosure lived only in manifest.json. This
3219	    helper renders the banner from that SAME serialized flag so report.md is honest on its own.
3220	
3221	    Returns the banner markdown ONLY when ``release_disclosure`` is a dict that explicitly
3222	    records ``adjudicated == False``. Returns "" when the disclosure is missing, malformed, or
3223	    records ``adjudicated`` truthy (a genuinely D8-judged release stays banner-free). The
3224	    helper asserts NO finding and touches NO faithfulness verdict — it is pure disclosure
3225	    plumbing.
3226	    """
3227	    if not isinstance(release_disclosure, dict):
3228	        return ""
3229	    # Strict identity (I-deepfix-001 Codex P2): emit the banner ONLY when
3230	    # adjudicated is EXPLICITLY False. A missing key or a malformed falsey value
3231	    # (None / 0 / "") must NOT trigger it — the contract is "explicit
3232	    # adjudicated == False", not "falsey".
3233	    if release_disclosure.get("adjudicated") is not False:
3234	        return ""
3235	    return (
3236	        "> **STRONGEST VERIFIER (four-role D8) DID NOT RUN for this run — findings are "
3237	        "UNVERIFIED-by-D8.**\n"

3244	    )

6603	    """I-wire-007 (#1321): RECOVER the partial 4-role verdicts settled before the outer seam wall fired.
6604	
6605	    The outer seam WALL (`run_honest_sweep_r3` ~12186) orphans the evaluation worker on a timeout, so
6606	    the in-memory `final_verdicts` are unreachable and the old handler discarded everything
6607	    (`final_verdicts={}`). The compute drain persists each SETTLED claim's final verdict to the
6608	    `four_role_settled_verdicts.jsonl` sidecar (sweep_integration), so on a seam timeout we read it back
6609	    here and return the partials already computed.
6610	
6611	    Returns `(final_verdicts, coverage_fraction, settled_count)` where:
6612	      * `final_verdicts` maps claim_id -> the UNCHANGED `_compose_final_verdict` verdict for every
6613	        SETTLED claim (the unsettled remainder is ABSENT -> stays UNSUPPORTED/held downstream).
6614	      * `coverage_fraction` is the CONSERVATIVE fail-closed proxy `verified_settled / total_claims`:
6615	        the denominator is ALL claims (not just the settled ones), so the unsettled remainder DRAGS
6616	        coverage DOWN — a partial set can therefore only route to released_with_disclosed_gaps / held,
6617	        never a false full-certify. (Distinct covered-element ids per VERIFIED claim are unioned so a
6618	        duplicated id is not double-counted; the proxy is still bounded by claim count.)
6619	      * `settled_count` is how many claims settled before the wall (for the log / disclosed gap).
6620	
6621	    FAIL-CLOSED + FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: this RECOVERS already-computed verdicts; it never SYNTHESIZES
6622	    one, never marks an unsettled claim VERIFIED, and never relaxes a threshold. On ANY read error it
6623	    returns `({}, 0.0, 0)` — byte-equivalent to the prior discard-everything behaviour (no worse).
6624	    """
6625	    empty: "tuple[dict[str, str], float, int]" = ({}, 0.0, 0)
6626	    if run_dir is None or total_claims <= 0:
6627	        return empty
6628	    try:
6629	        from src.polaris_graph.roles.sweep_integration import (  # noqa: PLC0415
6630	            FOUR_ROLE_SETTLED_VERDICTS_FILENAME,
6631	        )

6633	        return empty

6635	    if not sidecar.is_file():

6638	    verified_covered: set[str] = set()
6639	    try:
6640	        for line in sidecar.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():

6654	            final_verdicts[str(claim_id)] = str(verdict)

content.text: There's already a scoring-packer that strips scaffolding. Let me examine it and the appendix boundary.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Pack a POLARIS run's report.md into the official DeepResearch-Bench-II submission
3	layout (report/<model>/idx-<task>.md) so the official scorer (third_party/
4	DeepResearch-Bench-II/run_evaluation.py, Gemini rubric judge) can score it.
5	
6	The DRB-II scorer reads the markdown and truncates at MAX_PAPER_CHARS (=150000 per the
7	official .env_example); the run-#7 masthead-swallow (TI-22: a source masthead promoted to
8	a `# ` H1 + a ~95k-char swallowed line) silently consumed ~74% of the budget. This packer
9	removes ONLY provably-non-answer junk and FAILS LOUD on silent content loss:
10	
11	  - strips foreign source-masthead `# ` H1s (the report's own structure is `##`/`###`),
12	  - strips oversized swallowed lines (> PACK_DRB2_MAX_LINE_CHARS),
13	  - strips base64 / data-URI image payloads,
14	  - omits gap-stub META-TEXT (placeholder prose for already-dropped claims — not answer
15	    content; DeepTRACE/DRB-II would score it as on-topic-but-non-substantive),
16	  - FAILS LOUD (SystemExit) if the ANSWER BODY (everything before `## Bibliography` /
17	    `## Evidence-support disclosure`) still exceeds the scorer's truncation budget — so a
18	    silently-truncated answer is caught, never shipped as a complete-looking report,
19	  - asserts idx <-> task-id alignment (scoring report-X against task-Y's rubrics is the
20	    §-1.4 silently-wrong-number class).
21	
22	EVAL TOOLING ONLY. It never edits the scored claims or their citations — it removes only
23	provably-non-answer junk. Faithfulness is untouched. LAW VI: all caps are env-overridable.
24	"""
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import argparse
28	import json
29	import os
30	import re
31	import sys
32	from pathlib import Path
33	
34	MAX_PAPER_CHARS = int(os.getenv("PACK_DRB2_MAX_PAPER_CHARS", "150000"))
35	MAX_LINE_CHARS = int(os.getenv("PACK_DRB2_MAX_LINE_CHARS", "5000"))
36	
37	# I-beatboth-011 idx 15 (#1289): the DRB-II task catalog. A run's internal id (e.g. "task72") does
38	# NOT equal its DRB-II `idx` field (task72 -> idx 56), so packing AI-labor prose with the wrong
39	# `--idx 72` would score it against idx-72's task (Parkinson's) — a silent near-zero (§-1.4
40	# silently-wrong-number). The topic-overlap guard below resolves the task at `--idx` and refuses a
41	# report whose H1/opening shares ZERO content words with that task's (English) description.
42	_DEFAULT_TASKS_JSONL = (
43	    Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "third_party" / "DeepResearch-Bench-II" / "tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl"
44	)
45	# Content word for topic-overlap: a >=3-char alpha token (drops the structural stopwords). Lowercased.
46	_TOPIC_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z][a-z'-]{2,}")
47	_TOPIC_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
48	    "the", "and", "for", "with", "this", "that", "from", "into", "over", "under", "about", "review",
49	    "report", "research", "study", "analysis", "impact", "role", "case", "based", "using", "between",
50	    "their", "these", "those", "which", "while", "have", "has", "are", "was", "were", "its", "via",
51	})
52	
53	# A single-`#` H1 (`^#\s` — `## `/`### ` never match) is treated as a FOREIGN source
54	# masthead ONLY when it appears AFTER report body sections (`## `) have begun. POLARIS's own
55	# document title `# Research report` (which appears BEFORE any `## `) is KEPT (Codex over-strip
56	# catch: do not strip the report's own title).
57	_H1_RE = re.compile(r"^#\s+\S")
58	_BASE64_IMG_RE = re.compile(r"data:image/[^;]+;base64,", re.IGNORECASE)
59	# Gap-stub META-TEXT = a pipeline-internal disclosure about a slot/claim that did not verify.
60	# Keyed on PIPELINE-INTERNAL markers that never appear in legitimate answer prose (so a real
61	# sentence containing "was redacted"/"insufficient evidence" is NEVER stripped — Codex
62	# over-strip catch). Covers both observed stub templates: the "previously stated here"
63	# placeholder and the "did not survive {strict,4-role} verification ... curator-actionable gap
64	# ... this slot is a ..." contract-coverage disclosure.
65	_GAP_STUB_RE = re.compile(
66	    r"(?i)a claim previously stated here|"
67	    r"did not survive (strict|4-role|four-role|d8|provenance)[- ]?verification|"
68	    r"did not survive (strict|4-role|four-role) verification|"
69	    r"curator-actionable gap|human_gap_tasks|frame_coverage_report|"
70	    r"placeholder for (a|the) (claim|finding|sentence)|"
71	    r"this slot is a [a-z-]*\s*gap|"
72	    # N4 (I-deepfix-001 wave-2): plain-English gap disclosure (carries [N]).
73	    r"insufficient verified evidence"
74	)
75	# The answer-body boundary: the appendix sections that are NOT rubric-scored answer content.
76	# I-deepfix-001 (#1344): ALSO match the T5 audit-machinery appendix header the render emits
77	# (run_honest_sweep_r3._AUDIT_MACHINERY_APPENDIX_BOUNDARY: "## Appendix: audit, disclosure, and
78	# weighting (not scored as report claims)"). It carries a trailing parenthetical, so its branch
79	# allows any trailing content after "weighting" (the anchored `\s*$` on the other three stays).
80	# Without this branch the reliability/audit-counts block (moved to a TYPED trailing appendix so the
81	# scored body opens on a real claim) would fall INSIDE the scored answer, diluting recall / risking
82	# scorer truncation of real body content. Boundary/segmentation only — never drops any report bytes.
83	_APPENDIX_BOUNDARY_RE = re.compile(
84	    r"^##\s+(?:Bibliography|Evidence-support disclosure|References)\s*$"
85	    r"|^##\s+Appendix:\s*audit,\s*disclosure,\s*and\s*weighting\b.*$",
86	    re.IGNORECASE,
87	)
88	
89	
90	def strip_junk(report_md: str) -> tuple[str, dict]:
91	    """Return (cleaned_markdown, stats). Removes ONLY provably-non-answer junk: foreign
92	    source-masthead H1s (a `# ` heading appearing AFTER `## ` body sections — the report's own
93	    `# ` title is kept), oversized swallowed lines that are NOT markdown table rows, base64
94	    image payloads, and the specific gap-stub placeholder template. Deterministic; no LLM."""
95	    out_lines: list[str] = []
96	    stats = {"masthead_h1": 0, "oversized_lines": 0, "base64_lines": 0, "gap_stub_lines": 0}
97	    seen_h2 = False
98	    for line in report_md.splitlines():
99	        stripped = line.lstrip()
100	        if stripped.startswith("## "):
101	            seen_h2 = True
102	        # foreign masthead H1 — only AFTER body sections began (keep the report's own title).
103	        if _H1_RE.match(line) and seen_h2:
104	            stats["masthead_h1"] += 1
105	            continue
106	        # oversized swallowed line — but PRESERVE wide markdown table rows (`|`-led).
107	        if len(line) > MAX_LINE_CHARS and not stripped.startswith("|"):
108	            stats["oversized_lines"] += 1
109	            continue
110	        if _BASE64_IMG_RE.search(line):
111	            stats["base64_lines"] += 1
112	            continue
113	        if _GAP_STUB_RE.search(line):
114	            stats["gap_stub_lines"] += 1
115	            continue
116	        out_lines.append(line)
117	    return "\n".join(out_lines).strip() + "\n", stats
118	
119	
120	def answer_body(cleaned_md: str) -> str:
121	    """The rubric-scored answer = everything BEFORE the first appendix boundary
122	    (## Bibliography / ## Evidence-support disclosure). The scorer truncates the tail, so
123	    the answer must fit the budget for a fair, non-truncated score."""
124	    lines = cleaned_md.splitlines()
125	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
126	        if _APPENDIX_BOUNDARY_RE.match(line):
127	            return "\n".join(lines[:i]).strip() + "\n"
128	    return cleaned_md
129	
130	
131	def _topic_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
132	    """Lowercased content-word set for topic-overlap (drops structural stopwords)."""
133	    return {w for w in _TOPIC_WORD_RE.findall((text or "").lower()) if w not in _TOPIC_STOPWORDS}
134	
135	
136	def _load_drb2_tasks(tasks_jsonl: Path | None) -> list[dict]:
137	    """Load the DRB-II task catalog (or [] when the file is absent/unreadable — the topic guard then
138	    no-ops with a warning rather than blocking a legitimate run whose data file is not present)."""
139	    if not tasks_jsonl or not Path(tasks_jsonl).exists():
140	        return []
141	    out: list[dict] = []
142	    for line in Path(tasks_jsonl).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace").splitlines():
143	        line = line.strip()
144	        if not line:
145	            continue
146	        try:
147	            out.append(json.loads(line))
148	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
149	            continue
150	    return out
151	
152	
153	def _resolve_idx_for_task_name(task_name: str, records: list[dict]) -> str | None:
154	    """The DRB-II `idx` field of the record whose `id` == task_name (e.g. "task72" -> "56")."""
155	    for r in records:
156	        if str(r.get("id")) == str(task_name):
157	            return str(r.get("idx"))
158	    return None
159	
160	
161	def _report_topic_text(report_md: str) -> str:
162	    """The report's TOPIC surface: its first `# ` H1 plus the opening ~2000 chars of prose. Robust
163	    against a generic H1 — a correctly-paired report's opening always names its own subject."""
164	    h1 = ""
165	    for line in (report_md or "").splitlines():
166	        if _H1_RE.match(line):
167	            h1 = line.lstrip("#").strip()
168	            break
169	    return f"{h1}\n{(report_md or '')[:2000]}"
170	
171	
172	def _assert_drb2_topic_match(idx: int, report_md: str, records: list[dict]) -> None:
173	    """FAIL LOUD (SystemExit) if the report's topic shares ZERO content words with the DRB-II task at
174	    `idx` (resolved by the record's `idx` FIELD, the id<->idx offset). Uses the task's English
175	    `description` (robust to zh prompts). No-ops when the catalog is unavailable or either side yields
176	    no extractable topic words (never a false positive). Fires exactly on a wrong-idx mispairing
177	    (AI-labor report vs idx-72 Parkinson's = zero shared words); never on a correct run."""
178	    if not records:
179	        print("[pack_drb2] WARN: DRB-II task catalog unavailable; idx<->topic guard skipped.")
180	        return
181	    rec = next((r for r in records if str(r.get("idx")) == str(idx)), None)
182	    if rec is None:
183	        print(f"[pack_drb2] WARN: no DRB-II task with idx={idx} in the catalog; topic guard skipped.")
184	        return
185	    desc_words = _topic_words(str(rec.get("description") or ""))
186	    report_words = _topic_words(_report_topic_text(report_md))
187	    if not desc_words or not report_words:
188	        return  # cannot judge — do not false-positive
189	    if not (desc_words & report_words):
190	        raise SystemExit(
191	            f"[pack_drb2] FAIL-LOUD topic mismatch at --idx {idx}: the report shares ZERO content "
192	            f"words with DRB-II idx {idx} (id={rec.get('id')!r}, {str(rec.get('description'))[:90]!r}). "
193	            f"You are almost certainly packing against the WRONG idx — the id<->idx offset is real "
194	            f"(id 'task72' is idx 56, NOT 72). Pass the correct --idx (or --task-name). "
195	            f"(§-1.4 silently-wrong-number)"
196	        )
197	
198	
199	def pack_one(
200	    report_md_path: Path,
201	    idx: int,
202	    *,
203	    task_id: object | None = None,
204	    task_name: str | None = None,
205	    tasks_jsonl: Path | None = None,
206	    out_dir: Path,
207	    model: str = "polaris",
208	    strict_truncation: bool = True,
209	) -> Path:
210	    """Pack ONE report.md into out_dir/<model>/idx-<idx>.md. Returns the written path.
211	
212	    `task_id` (the DRB-II tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl idx) must equal `idx` — a mismatch means
213	    we would score this report against the WRONG task's rubrics (FAIL LOUD).
214	
215	    I-beatboth-011 idx 15 (#1289): two stronger guards close the silently-wrong-idx landmine the
216	    `task_id == idx` check misses (it passes when BOTH are wrong-but-equal):
217	      (A) `task_name` (e.g. "task72") is resolved to its DRB-II `idx` field via the catalog and
218	          asserted == `idx`, so the real id<->idx offset (task72 -> idx 56) is enforced in code.
219	      (B) a topic-overlap guard refuses a report whose opening shares zero content words with the
220	          task at `idx` (the AI-labor-vs-Parkinson's mispairing)."""
221	    if task_id is not None and int(task_id) != int(idx):
222	        raise SystemExit(
223	            f"[pack_drb2] FAIL-LOUD idx<->task mismatch: idx={idx} but task_id={task_id}. "
224	            f"Scoring against the wrong rubrics is forbidden (§-1.4 silently-wrong-number)."
225	        )
226	    records = _load_drb2_tasks(tasks_jsonl)
227	    # (A) id<->idx resolution: a `--task-name` must map (by the catalog's `idx` field) to `--idx`.
228	    if task_name is not None:
229	        resolved = _resolve_idx_for_task_name(task_name, records)
230	        if resolved is None and records:
231	            raise SystemExit(
232	                f"[pack_drb2] FAIL-LOUD: --task-name {task_name!r} not found in the DRB-II catalog."
233	            )
234	        if resolved is not None and str(resolved) != str(idx):
235	            raise SystemExit(
236	                f"[pack_drb2] FAIL-LOUD id<->idx offset: --task-name {task_name!r} resolves to DRB-II "
237	                f"idx {resolved}, but --idx {idx} was given. The run id ('task72') is NOT the idx "
238	                f"(56) — pass --idx {resolved}. (§-1.4 silently-wrong-number)"
239	            )
240	    report_md = report_md_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
241	    # (B) topic-overlap belt-and-suspenders (fires on a wrong-idx mispairing, never on a correct run).
242	    _assert_drb2_topic_match(idx, report_md, records)
243	    cleaned, stats = strip_junk(report_md)
244	    body = answer_body(cleaned)
245	    if strict_truncation and len(body) > MAX_PAPER_CHARS:
246	        raise SystemExit(
247	            f"[pack_drb2] FAIL-LOUD: answer body {len(body)} chars > scorer budget "
248	            f"{MAX_PAPER_CHARS} even after junk-strip {stats} — the scorer would silently "
249	            f"truncate scored content (the run-#7 bug). Fix the generator/render (shorten "
250	            f"the answer or move appendices) before submitting; set strict_truncation=False "
251	            f"only to MEASURE the truncation, never to hide it."
252	        )
253	    out = out_dir / model / f"idx-{idx}.md"
254	    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
255	    out.write_text(cleaned, encoding="utf-8")
256	    return out
257	
258	
259	def main() -> None:
260	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Pack a POLARIS report.md for DeepResearch-Bench-II.")
261	    ap.add_argument("--report", required=True, help="path to POLARIS report.md")
262	    ap.add_argument("--idx", required=True, type=int, help="DRB-II task idx (the catalog `idx` field, NOT the run id number)")
263	    ap.add_argument("--task-id", type=int, default=None, help="tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl idx (asserted == --idx)")
264	    ap.add_argument("--task-name", default=None, help="DRB-II task id e.g. 'task72' (resolved to its idx via the catalog and asserted == --idx)")
265	    ap.add_argument("--tasks-jsonl", default=str(_DEFAULT_TASKS_JSONL), help="DRB-II tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl (for the id<->idx + topic-overlap guards)")
266	    ap.add_argument("--out-dir", default="report", help="output root (report/<model>/idx-N.md)")
267	    ap.add_argument("--model", default="polaris")
268	    ap.add_argument("--allow-truncation", action="store_true", help="measure-only: do NOT fail on over-budget body")
269	    args = ap.parse_args()
270	    out = pack_one(
271	        Path(args.report), args.idx, task_id=args.task_id,
272	        task_name=args.task_name, tasks_jsonl=Path(args.tasks_jsonl) if args.tasks_jsonl else None,
273	        out_dir=Path(args.out_dir), model=args.model,
274	        strict_truncation=not args.allow_truncation,
275	    )
276	    print(f"[pack_drb2] wrote {out} ({out.stat().st_size} bytes)")
277	
278	
279	if __name__ == "__main__":
280	    main()
281

input.description: Read journal_only_filter header and flags

content.content:
"""journal_only corpus-quality filter (I-ready-017 #1134).

A flag-gated, fail-closed filter that restricts the citeable corpus to
peer-reviewed JOURNAL ARTICLES when a protocol declares
``source_restriction: journal_only`` (e.g. the drb_72 question: "Ensure the
review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.").

Design (Codex APPROVE, design gate iter-4, #1134):

- ONE predicate ``is_citeable_journal`` is the single source of truth for
  "is this a citeable journal article?". It is used by every ingress filter
  AND by the final no-leak assertion, so the partition and the backstop can
  never disagree.
- A SINGLE upstream source-filter on ``retrieval.evidence_rows`` +
  ``classified_sources`` (applied after the last retrieval stage, before any
  consumer) means selection, the generator's internal retrieval
  (``live_corpus``/M-52), and all contradiction/disclosure detectors inherit
  journal-only by construction — no per-consumer chasing.
- The V30 report-contract carries entities through plan slots / entity maps /
  anchors INDEPENDENTLY of the prepended evidence rows, so those are pruned
  too; a ``required: true`` slot bound to a non-journal entity ABORTS.
- A final no-leak assertion on the generator's evidence pool is the
  structural backstop.

EVERYTHING here is inert unless BOTH ``PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY=1``
AND the protocol declares ``source_restriction: journal_only``. Default-OFF is
byte-identical: callers gate on ``journal_only_active(protocol)`` before
invoking any filter, and the metadata sidecar is only populated on the ON path.

Fail-closed throughout: an UNCERTAIN row (no proven journal-article signal) is
EXCLUDED, never admitted. A required non-journal contract slot, or any
non-journal row reaching the generator, ABORTS rather than silently degrades
(LAW II).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
from dataclasses import dataclass, field, is_dataclass, replace as _dc_replace
from typing import Any, Iterable, Mapping, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlsplit, urlunsplit

# ── Flag + protocol gating ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

JOURNAL_ONLY_FLAG = "PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY"
JOURNAL_ONLY_RESTRICTION = "journal_only"

# Citeable journal tiers. T1 = peer-reviewed primary; T2 = systematic review /
# meta-analysis (itself a journal article). All other tiers are non-journal.
CITEABLE_JOURNAL_TIERS = frozenset({"T1", "T2"})

# URL path fragments that mark a navigation / search / issue / table-of-contents
# / homepage surface rather than a single article. Rejected even on a journal
# domain (Codex design P1-2: domain membership alone never blesses a row).
_NON_ARTICLE_URL_MARKERS = (
    "/search", "/search?", "?search=", "/find", "/browse", "/issue", "/issues",
    "/toc", "/current", "/archive", "/aim-and-scope", "/editorial-board",
    "/most-read", "/collections", "/topics", "/journals/", "/journal/",
)

8818

8821	         location ``run_gate_e2e`` writes; the monitored-live-job path) — loaded
8822	         verbatim so its pinned ``contract_sha256`` is honored, NOT recomputed;
8823	      2. else compile it via ``research_planning_gate.run_research_planning_gate``
8824	         (mode="autonomous", the small policy compiler LLM — fast, spend-bounded)
8825	         and pin it to that same path.
8826	
8827	    Returns the ``PlanningGateArtifact`` or ``None`` on any failure (fail-open:
8828	    the seam then keeps the champion path, byte-identical). This is called ONLY
8829	    under ``PG_GATE`` — with the flag unset it never runs.
8830	    """
8831	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import artifact_from_dict
8832	
8833	    art_path = run_dir / "planning_gate_artifact.json"
8834	
8835	    # (1) a pre-pinned artifact — the canonical run_gate_e2e location.
8836	    if art_path.exists():
8837	        try:
8838	            artifact = artifact_from_dict(json.loads(art_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")))
8839	            log(f"[planning_gate] S2 loaded pinned artifact "
8840	                f"contract_sha256={(artifact.contract_sha256 or '')[:12]} "
8841	                f"(hard_terms={len(artifact.contract.hard_terms())})")
8842	            return artifact
8843	        except Exception as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open to compile / champion
8844	            log(f"[planning_gate] S2 pinned artifact unreadable, will compile: {_exc}")
8845	
8846	    # (2) compile + pin. Autonomous mode NEVER blocks (no input channel here).
8847	    try:
8848	        import asyncio as _asyncio
8849	        from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planning_gate import (
8850	            run_research_planning_gate,
8851	        )
8852

8855	                run_research_planning_gate(prompt, mode="autonomous", run_id=run_id)
8856	            )
8857	
8858	        import concurrent.futures as _futures
8859	        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as _pool:
8860	            gate = _pool.submit(_compile).result()
8861	        artifact = gate.artifact
8862	        try:

8870	            f"contract_sha256={(artifact.contract_sha256 or '')[:12]} "
8871	            f"(hard_terms={len(artifact.contract.hard_terms())})")
8872	        return artifact
8873	    except Exception as _exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open to champion path

518	
519	    Three modes:

521	      * dry_e2e         — drive the FULL live harness path (gate offline stub + _run_fresh_e2e)
522	                          but with ``e2e_runner`` = a NETWORK-MOCKED run_one_query stub. Proves
523	                          the whole wiring offline; NO network, NO spend. The gate also runs on
524	                          the offline compiler stub (live=False) so no OPENROUTER call fires.
525	      * live            — the real fresh e2e (network + spend).
526	    """

532	    # The gate fires its real OpenRouter policy model ONLY on a true live run; dry AND dry_e2e
533	    # both use the offline compiler stub so the test/mode is fully spend-free.

555	            "n_terms": len(artifact.contract.all_terms()),
556	            "n_hard": len(artifact.contract.hard_terms()),

563	        }

565	        # --- STAGE 2: retrieval / compose-render / audit WIRING projections ---
566	        d["stages"]["retrieval_wiring"] = _retrieval_wiring_proof(artifact, prompt)
567	        d["stages"]["compose_render_wiring"] = _compose_render_wiring_proof(artifact)
568	
569	        # --- STAGE 3: the assembled fresh-e2e pipeline call ---
570	        assembled = _assembled_pipeline_call(q, out_root, gate_on=gate_on, mode=mode)
571	        d["stages"]["fresh_e2e_call"] = assembled
572	
573	        if not take_live_branch:
574	            # Prove the audit wiring against a stub report (no compose). The REAL audit runs
575	            # on the fresh report.md in the live path below.
576	            stub_report = f"# {prompt[:50]}\n\n## Introduction\n\nStub.\n\n## References\n"
577	            audit_ok = True
578	            try:
579	                d["stages"]["audit"] = {
580	                    "on_stub_report": True,
581	                    "audit": _run_audit(
582	                        artifact, stub_report,
583	                        retrieval_scope_status="not_evaluated_dry_smoke"),
584	                }
585	            except Exception as _e:  # noqa: BLE001
586	                audit_ok = False
587	                d["stages"]["audit"] = {"on_stub_report": True, "error": str(_e)}
588	            # The DRY-SMOKE invariant is that the harness ASSEMBLES a complete, gate-fired
589	            # pipeline call — NOT that the deterministic offline stub emits rich queries (it
590	            # is deliberately minimal). We assert: (1) the gate pinned an artifact and the
591	            # autonomous invariant held; (2) all three projections compiled without error;
592	            # (3) the env slate + query dict assembled with PG_GATE set; (4) the audit wired.
593	            # NO-STARVATION at planning level: the gate candidate-query count can NEVER be
594	            # below the no-gate baseline (which adds 0 lanes) — gate_count >= 0 always holds,
595	            # and for the hard-scope canary (task 72) the journal/English constraint MUST
596	            # reach the projection as >=1 scope-anchored amplified query.
597	            rw = d["stages"]["retrieval_wiring"]
598	            no_starvation_ok = rw["candidate_query_count"] >= 0  # gate >= no_gate(=0)
599	            hard_scope_reached = (
600	                d["stages"]["gate"]["n_hard"] == 0  # no hard terms -> nothing to route
601	                or rw["amplified_query_count"] >= 1  # a hard term -> a scope-anchored lane
602	                or any(sz > 0 for sz in rw["scope_lane_sizes"].values()))
603	            d["stages"]["fresh_e2e_call"]["no_starvation_ok"] = no_starvation_ok
604	            d["stages"]["fresh_e2e_call"]["hard_scope_reached_projection"] = hard_scope_reached
605	            d["assembled_ok"] = bool(
606	                d["stages"]["gate"]["autonomous_ok"]
607	                and no_starvation_ok
608	                and hard_scope_reached
609	                and audit_ok
610	                and assembled["env_slate"]["PG_GATE"] in ("0", "1")
611	                and assembled["query_dict"]["question_head"])
612	        else:
613	            # --- LIVE: execute the fresh e2e, then audit + score the REAL report.md ---
614	            t1 = time.time()
615	            sweep_run_dir, sweep_summary = _run_fresh_e2e(
616	                q, out_root, gate_on=gate_on, mode=mode, runner=e2e_runner)
617	            report_path = sweep_run_dir / "report.md"
618	            sweep_status = sweep_summary.get("status")
619	            aborted = _is_abort_status(sweep_status)
620	            d["stages"]["fresh_e2e"] = {
621	                "elapsed_s": round(time.time() - t1, 1),
622	                "sweep_run_dir": str(sweep_run_dir),
623	                "report_exists": report_path.exists(),
624	                "sweep_status": sweep_status,
625	                "sweep_error": sweep_summary.get("error") or "",
626	                "aborted": aborted,
627	            }
628	            # FAIL-LOUD: a run that ABORTED (scope reject, safety refusal, no-sources,
629	            # corpus-inadequate, budget, ...) or produced NO report.md is NOT a scoreable
630	            # report — even though the abort path writes a STUB report.md. Do NOT audit/score
631	            # a stub; set d["error"] so per-draw ok is False and main() exits non-zero.

635	                    f"{sweep_summary.get('error') or ''}".strip()
636	                )
637	            elif not report_path.exists():

639	            else:

641	                # co-locate the judged report + artifact + audit in the harness run dir
642	                (run_dir / "report.md").write_text(report_text, encoding="utf-8")
643	                biblio_path = sweep_run_dir / "bibliography.json"
644	                audit = _run_audit(
645	                    artifact, report_text,
646	                    retrieval_scope_status="fresh_gate_scoped" if gate_on else "fresh_no_gate")
647	                (run_dir / "contract_compliance.json").write_text(
648	                    json.dumps(audit, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
649	                d["stages"]["audit"] = {"counts": audit.get("counts"),
650	                                        "retrieval_scope_status": audit.get("retrieval_scope_status")}
651	                if score_race:
652	                    d["stages"]["race"] = _run_cmd(_race_command(run_dir / "report.md", task_id))
653	                if score_fact:
654	                    d["stages"]["fact"] = _run_cmd(_fact_command(sweep_run_dir, task_id))
655	                _ = biblio_path  # referenced for provenance; audit already parses report refs
656	                d["live_ok"] = True
657	
658	        (run_dir / "gate_e2e_telemetry.json").write_text(
659	            json.dumps(d, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
660	        per_task["draws"].append(d)
661	
662	    return per_task

1	> **STRONGEST VERIFIER (four-role D8) DID NOT RUN for this run — findings are UNVERIFIED-by-D8.**
2	>
3	> The four-role D8 adjudication (the strongest faithfulness verifier) did not bind for this run, so the findings below carry only the strict_verify / span-grounding / NLI evidence — NOT the final D8 adjudication. Treat them as UNVERIFIED-by-D8 pending a re-judge. See `manifest.json` (`release_disclosure`) for the per-run disclosure detail.
4	
5	## Key Findings
6	
7	_Each finding below is verbatim text carried up from a cited body span; it passes strict_verify (span bounds + numeric match + ≥2 content-word grounding) but is single-origin unless marked corroborated, and span-grounding is NOT a peer-reviewed or on-topic guarantee. Citations are the body's._
8	
9	Job Displacement by AI Technologies.** AI's dual impact on the labor market—simultaneously displacing certain roles while creating new ones—has emerged as a central theme in recent research.[10][2]
10	**Data Gaps and Forecasting Uncertainties in AI Labor Studies.** Artificial intelligence is still loosely defined in the public imagination and crosscutting in ways that can make it difficult to discern informed insight from speculation.[4]
11	**Synthesis of AI Labor Market Restructuring Impacts.** Taken together, the converging evidence suggests that AI is restructuring labor markets primarily through task replacement rather than wholesale job displacement, while simultaneously raising skill requirements and creating wage polarization between AI-augmented and AI-displaced workers.[8][17][20]
12	**AI and the Fourth Industrial Revolution Context.** Controlled field experiments document large productivity gains across writing, customer support, software development, accounting, law, and translation, with studies reporting 15% to more than 50% reductions in task-completion time, alongside disproportionately large benefits for less-experienced workers producing "skill compression" within occupations.[3]
13	**Industry-Specific Impacts and Workforce Transitions.** In the financial sector, AI has been particularly transformative, as AI-powered algorithms now process vast amounts of data to generate personalized investment strategies—tasks once exclusive to human financial advisors—and AI-driven risk prediction models have reshaped how institutions assess market crises.[15]
14	**How AI Disrupts Labor Markets and Modifies Work Tasks.** As a result of AI, some skills can be increasingly replicated by technologies, particularly manual and fine psychomotor abilities.[7]
15	
16	### AI and the Fourth Industrial Revolution Context
17	
18	The term Industry 4.0 was coined by German Professor Wolfgang Wahlster in 2011 at the Hannover Fair, and it represents the digital transformation of manufacturing industries focused on automation, interconnectivity, and real-time process optimization.[1] Global business investment in AI increased 13-fold from US$14.57 billion in 2013 to US$189 billion in 2023, with 35% of businesses already using AI in their operations and more than 50% intending to implement AI technologies.[2] By late 2024, nearly 40% of U.S. adults ages 18–64 reported using AI tools, a pace of adoption that exceeds comparable stages for personal computers and the internet.[3] Furthermore, a report published by NANDA at MIT finds that of the tens of billions of dollars that private enterprises have invested, 95% of them reported no return.[4] AI's impact on the labor market operates primarily at the task level rather than displacing whole occupations; when AI can perform most tasks that make up a particular job, the share of people in that role within a company falls by about 14%, but when AI's impact is concentrated in just a few tasks, employment in that role can grow.[5] Controlled field experiments document large productivity gains across writing, customer support, software development, accounting, law, and translation, with studies reporting 15% to more than 50% reductions in task-completion time, alongside disproportionately large benefits for less-experienced workers producing "skill compression" within occupations.[3] In high-income countries, defined in 2024 as those with a gross national income per capita above US$13,935, 3.5% of men's jobs fall into the highest automation risk category.[6] A study of U.S. Census Bureau data reported that only 5.4% of firms were using AI technologies in the spring of 2024, up from 3.7% the year prior, and researchers estimate that the time savings AI currently provides amounts to only 1.4% of work hours.[4] The potential for automation to disrupt employment is uneven across demographic groups: in 2023, the share of jobs with high automation potential was 7.8% for women compared to 2.9% for men, and by 2025 these figures had risen to 9.6% for women and 3.5% for men, reflecting women's greater concentration in service and administrative sectors with routine tasks.[6]
19	
20	### How AI Disrupts Labor Markets and Modifies Work Tasks
21	
22	As a result of AI, some skills can be increasingly replicated by technologies, particularly manual and fine psychomotor abilities.[7] AI simultaneously increases the demand for both skills required to develop AI systems and skills to use AI applications.[7] Empirical evidence from a systematic review following PRISMA 2020 guidelines, which screened 1,847 initial records and retained 94 studies for qualitative synthesis, documents a 14–41% reduction in postings for entry- and mid-level software development and content-creation roles in high-income economies between 2022 and 2024.[8] A systematic review of 432 research studies published since 2018, focusing on the 154 most relevant works, uncovers that AI is simultaneously displacing routine jobs and creating new high-skill roles, demanding urgent policy and retraining responses.[9]
23	
24	### Job Creation vs. Job Displacement by AI Technologies
25	
26	AI's dual impact on the labor market—simultaneously displacing certain roles while creating new ones—has emerged as a central theme in recent research.[10][2] A bibliometric analysis finds that AI automation displaces low-skilled jobs while generating new high-skill positions, particularly in data science and machine learning.[10] Research tracking AI adoption from 2010 to 2023 finds that when AI can perform most tasks comprising a particular job, the share of people in that role within a company falls by about 14%, but when AI's impact is concentrated in just a few tasks, employment in that role can grow.[5] Workers in high-wage roles heavily exposed to AI saw their share of total employment grow by about 3% over five years, as AI boosted firm productivity and companies that used the technology grew faster.[5] In contrast, business, financial, architecture, and engineering jobs shrank by about 2% to 2.5% over five years due to their high share of tasks matching AI capabilities.[5] A U.S.-based study distinguishing between automation AI and augmentation AI finds that automation AI negatively impacts new work, employment, and wages in low-skilled occupations, while augmentation AI fosters the emergence of new work and raises wages for high-skilled occupations.[11] Since ChatGPT's release in fall 2022, total U.S. employment increased approximately 2.5%, though employment in computer systems design and related services declined 5%, and employment fell 1% in the 10% of sectors most exposed to AI.[12] Wage data reveal a contrasting pattern: nominal average weekly wages nationwide increased 7.5% since fall 2022, while the computer systems design sector saw wages rise 16.7%, and among the top 10% of AI-exposed industries wages grew 8.5%.[12] Around 54% of workers could experience changes in their job responsibilities through the integration of generative AI tools, with broader AI adoption potentially contributing between 0.5% to 2.0% of annual GDP growth.[13] Between 2015 and 2022, 2159 instances of new work were added within occupations, averaging 308 new work additions per year.[11]
27	
28	**Tension** In contrast, business, financial, architecture, and engineering jobs shrank by about 2% to 2.5% over five years due to their high share of tasks matching AI capabilities.[5]
29	
30	### Industry-Specific Impacts and Workforce Transitions
31	
32	AI's restructuring of the labor market varies significantly across industries, with cross-sectoral survey data from 2022–2023 involving 22,816 employees, 1,684 business leaders, and 803 corporations revealing that AI primarily reshapes job functions rather than eliminating entire occupations, as 57% of respondents reported task augmentation while 36% expressed concern about job loss.[14] In the financial sector, AI has been particularly transformative, as AI-powered algorithms now process vast amounts of data to generate personalized investment strategies—tasks once exclusive to human financial advisors—and AI-driven risk prediction models have reshaped how institutions assess market crises.[15] Industry-level heterogeneity is substantial: the financial sector reports the highest work-related GenAI adoption at 63%, followed by professional services at 62%, with manufacturing experiencing the strongest year-on-year growth at approximately 58% (14.5 percentage points).[16] AI applications in manufacturing include predictive maintenance, predictive analytics, inventory management, machine vision, industrial robotics, and supply chain management.[1] Routine-based sectors such as manufacturing and customer service face higher displacement risks, while knowledge-based professions including healthcare, education, and creative industries are also affected by AI-driven changes.[14][2] Surveys through 2025 show that about 18% of U.S. firms have adopted AI, with work-related generative AI adoption reaching 41% of the workforce, while 78% of the labor force works at firms that have adopted AI and 54% at firms using large language models.[16]
33	
34	### Inequality and Skill-Biased Technological Change
35	
36	Among Korean firms that adopted AI, 56.5% report that AI has replaced specific tasks within existing jobs, while 32.2% say AI has increased the kinds of skills required and 38.3% say it has increased the level of skills required.[17] However, faster or more job-substitution-driven AI adoption could lead to greater inequality, making policy, education, and business adaptation critical.[18] Unchecked, economic activity surrounding the rollout of AI technologies could dampen economic growth by reducing employment dynamism and contributing to a greater concentration of wealth at the top, alongside sizeable environmental costs.[4] The occupations most exposed to AI tend to include professionals, managers, and science and engineering professionals, whereas occupations requiring manual skills and strength, such as cleaners, agricultural forestry and fishery labourers, and food preparation assistants, are the least exposed to AI.[17] Historical parallels suggest that past innovation waves displaced some jobs but increased productivity; during the first industrial revolution, real output per worker grew by about 0.84% annually between 1800 and 1850, while agricultural employment fell from roughly 75% to just over 50%.[18] Cross-country differences in AI adoption by SMEs range from 27% in Japan to 51% in Germany, with Korea on the lower end at 31%.[17]
37	
38	**Tension** However, faster or more job-substitution-driven AI adoption could lead to greater inequality, making policy, education, and business adaptation critical.[18]
39

42	Corpus: Serper + Semantic Scholar + OpenAlex live retrieval, augmented by domain backends (workforce: content_relevance_judge: scored=175 relevant=175 demoted=0 escalated=0 (weight-not-filter, no drop)).

50	Corpus adequacy: decision=proceed, 8/8 thresholds met.
51	Completeness checklist: 0/0 topics covered.
52	Corroboration counting: entailment verification (judge: z-ai/glm-5.2) was available for all 93 basket member(s); no corroboration verdicts were lost to judge unavailability.
53

A further 3 record(s) were screened as possible metric mismatches (the detector declined to assert a shared-metric contradiction) and withheld from this list; they remain in `contradictions.json`. The detector does NOT adjudicate by endpoint, population, timepoint, or source tier; raw detector output is available in `contradictions.json`. Per-flag enumeration (PT08 disclosure):

def _is_abort_status(status: str | None) -> bool:
    """True iff run_one_query's terminal status denotes a non-scoreable abort/failure."""
    s = (status or "").strip().lower()
    return any(s.startswith(p) for p in _ABORT_STATUS_PREFIXES)


def _run_fresh_e2e(
    q: dict, out_root: Path, *, gate_on: bool, mode: str, runner: Any = None,
) -> tuple[Path, dict]:
    """EXECUTE the fresh e2e. Sets the gate env slate, calls run_one_query DIRECTLY, returns
    the run dir that holds report.md.

    ``runner`` is the coroutine function invoked as ``runner(q, out_root)``. It defaults to the
    REAL ``scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query`` (the live path). The --dry-e2e mode and
    the offline integration test inject a NETWORK-MOCKED stub with the SAME (q, out_root) ->
    summary-dict interface, so the FULL harness path is exercised offline against run_one_query's
    real contract: same env slate, same q-dict, same report.md discovery, same abort detection.

    WHY run_one_query (NOT run_gate_b_query): run_gate_b_query FORCES
    ``PG_BENCHMARK_OFFICIAL_QUESTION=1`` and rebinds ``q["question"]`` to the DeepResearch-
    Bench-II canonical gold question by reading ``third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/
    tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl`` via gate0_lineage — a file this repo does NOT ship (we score
    against DRB v1, task 72). That path fail-loud-raises GateZeroLineageError before any
    retrieval. ``run_one_query`` is the DIRECT home of the S2 gate hook
    (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:10435-10468) and keys ALL of retrieval/generation/title on
    ``q["question"]`` — the VERBATIM DRB-v1 task-72 prompt supplied in ``q`` — with NO
    DRB-II coupling (the only gate0_lineage import inside run_one_query is guarded by
    ``resume=True``, which we never set). With ``PG_GATE=1`` + ``PG_USE_RESEARCH_PLANNER=1``
    threaded by ``_fresh_e2e_env_slate`` below, run_one_query builds ``_research_plan`` and
    the S2 hook projects the RetrievalProjection into FS-Researcher (the no-starvation path);
    with PG_GATE=0 the hook stays byte-identical to champion. run_one_query writes
    ``report.md`` (+ manifest.json/protocol.json) to ``out_root/<domain>/<slug>/``.

    Retrieval breadth (PG_SWEEP_* — 12/12/40) is applied by the real-run env, NOT here; this
    helper threads ONLY the gate switches so the byte-identical control (--no-gate) holds.
    """
    slate = _fresh_e2e_env_slate(gate_on=gate_on, mode=mode)
    for k, v in slate.items():
        os.environ[k] = v
    if runner is None:
        from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import run_one_query as runner
    # Capture run_one_query's summary — its ``status`` is the authoritative abort/success
    # signal. A scope reject (or any abort) returns status=abort_* AFTER writing a stub
    # report.md, so we can NOT infer success from report.md alone (that was the false-OK bug).
    summary = asyncio.run(runner(q, out_root))
    if not isinstance(summary, dict):
        summary = {"status": "error_no_summary", "error": "run_one_query returned non-dict"}
    return out_root / q["domain"] / q["slug"], summary

All verification complete. Here is the full fix plan.

---

# FIX PLAN — gate-inversion run: from RACE 0.3568 back above the 0.4447 champion
**Auditor:** Fable 5 (independent; no coordination). Branch `gate-inversion` @ `78fe2ca`, worktree `/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent`. All line numbers verified against the working tree and the actual run artifacts (`outputs/gate_e2e_final2/…`).

## 0. THREE CORRECTIONS TO THE FINDINGS PACK (verified against run artifacts — these change the plan)

**C1 — The quality mask NEVER ARMED; the 50-source mask was 100% TOPICALITY, not quality-UNKNOWN.**
`outputs/gate_e2e_final2/workforce/drb_72_ai_labor/eligibility_receipts.json` contains exactly 143 receipts, ALL `stage=topicality_eligibility` (93 pass / 50 fail, every exclusion basis `topicality cosine <x> < floor 0.300`). Zero quality receipts exist. The pack's claim that `quality_eligibility.py:226` UNKNOWN-fail-closed drove the over-mask is wrong *for this run* — `build_quality_eligibility` returned an EMPTY plan because the policy's `quality_profile` was `None` at run time. Root cause is C2. (The UNKNOWN-fail-closed hazard is still real — it becomes the #1 over-mask driver the moment C2 is fixed, with 41% UNKNOWN tier in corpus — so it still needs a fix, pre-emptively.)

**C2 — THE KEYSTONE BUG: the S1-pinned contract never steered the run; the sweep silently recompiled a degenerate contract.**
- `scripts/run_gate_e2e.py:542` sets `run_dir = out_root/<domain>/<slug>/draw{N}` and `:550-551` writes the pinned artifact to `draw1/planning_gate_artifact.json` (contract `04c81bd7e68f`, with `scope.source_types=peer_reviewed_journal` HARD, `scope.source_quality=high` HARD, `scope.source_languages=en` HARD — verified in `draw1/planning_gate_artifact.json`).
- But `_run_fresh_e2e` (`run_gate_e2e.py:366-407`) calls `run_one_query(q, out_root)`, whose run dir is `out_root/<domain>/<slug>/` (the TASK level). `_gate_load_or_compile_artifact` (`scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:8816-8875`, path check at `:8833-8836`) finds no artifact there and RE-COMPILES at the Phase-C seam (log `:2628`: `S2 compiled + pinned artifact contract_sha256=ad7638434434`, timestamped 02:25:28 vs the gate stage's 02:16:25). The recompiled contract (task-level `planning_gate_artifact.json`, verified) uses a different dimension vocabulary (`source_type=None/open`, `source_language=None/open`, NO `scope.source_quality`) — **the journal-only / high-quality / English terms were silently dropped.** I reproduced offline: compiling the *draw1* artifact through `retrieval_projection.from_artifact(...).to_retrieval_policy()` yields `quality_profile='high'`, `predicate_force={'quality_profile':'hard', 'allowed_source_kinds':'hard', 'languages':'hard'}`, and the quality mask fires correctly. So the code is fine; **the artifact hand-off is broken.** This single bug explains: the journal-only leak (nothing enforced), `contract_compliance.json` all-7-UNKNOWN, and bridge log `:2629` `facets=0, timeline_strictness=weight`.
- Additionally, the S2 retrieval projection hook (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py:10583-10640`) is reachable ONLY under `_fs_researcher_enabled()` (`PG_QGEN_FS_RESEARCHER`, default OFF at `:12872`) — unset in this run (env slate was only `PG_GATE/PG_GATE_MODE/PG_USE_RESEARCH_PLANNER`, verified in telemetry), so the gate's scope never reached query generation either. No `[planning_gate] S2 retrieval projection ON` line exists in the log.

**C3 — The D8 banner is NOT caused by the winner-gate bypass; it fires on this entrypoint unconditionally.**
The four-role D8 seam activates only when `PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE=1` AND an injected `four_role_transport` (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py:18565-18604`; `run_one_query` params at `:8964-8970`). `run_gate_e2e.py` never sets the mode nor injects a transport (its env slate at `:317-333` sets only the 3 gate vars), so every gate-e2e run takes the "legacy single-evaluator path" (log `:3280` `[eval_gate] … role=BINDING (legacy single-evaluator path)`) → `adjudicated=False` → banner. Fixing the reranker and re-enabling the winner gate is necessary but NOT sufficient — **the banner returns on every re-run until D8 is wired into this entrypoint.** Also: the "dead reranker" was **W5** (`content_relevance_judge`'s Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B, OOM because foreign PID 1872629 held 75.25/79.25 GB — log `:2393`); the **W7** selection reranker was simply OFF (`PG_RERANKER_MODEL` unset — no `[select]` rerank or `[winner-id]` lines in the log; gate enable check at `evidence_selector.py:2670-2672`). GPU is now free (0/81920 MiB) so both load.

---

## RANKED FIX PLAN

### FIX 1 (highest leverage) — Artifact hand-off: make the pinned contract the one the sweep runs
**(a) `scripts/run_gate_e2e.py` : `_run_one_task` : after line 551 (and before `_run_fresh_e2e` at :615)** — also write the pinned artifact to the SWEEP's run dir:
```python
sweep_dir = out_root / q["domain"] / q["slug"]
sweep_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(sweep_dir / "planning_gate_artifact.json").write_text(
    json.dumps(artifact.to_dict(), ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
```
Per-draw: overwrite before each draw's `_run_fresh_e2e` (draws share the sweep dir).
**(b) `scripts/run_gate_e2e.py` : `_run_one_task` : after `_run_fresh_e2e` returns (~:617)** — fail-loud contract-identity guard: read back `sweep_dir/planning_gate_artifact.json`, assert its `contract_sha256` equals the gate stage's (`d["stages"]["gate"]["contract_sha256"]`); on mismatch set `d["error"]` (the run recompiled — never score it silently).
**(c) `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py` : `_gate_load_or_compile_artifact` : lines 8846-8872** — when the compile branch (2) fires, stamp it: `log` at WARNING with the phrase `RECOMPILED AT SEAM (pinned artifact absent)` and record `manifest["gate_contract_recompiled_at_seam"]=True` via the returned artifact (or a module sentinel read at manifest build). A silently swapped contract is exactly the §-1.4 silently-wrong-number class.
**Rationale:** restores the entire point of the gate: the HARD `peer_reviewed_journal`/`high`/`en` terms reach Phase C (`:14238` compiles the REAL policy → quality mask arms, receipts emitted), the outline feed, and the S4 audit (`run_gate_e2e.py:644-646` audits against the SAME contract the run obeyed — no more all-UNKNOWN).
**Faithfulness:** pure planning-input wiring, upstream of everything; `provenance_generator.py`/strict_verify untouched (0-diff).
**RACE impact:** prerequisite for every IF gain in Fix 4/5; by itself turns the S4 audit meaningful. No direct dimension delta until paired with Fix 5 (the armed quality mask would otherwise over-mask — do NOT ship Fix 1 without Fix 5).

### FIX 2 — Permanently fix the scoring-cache bug (measurement integrity)
**(a) `scripts/score_report_race.py` : `main` : insert after the raw write at lines 66-68:**
```python
cleaned = DRB / "data/test_data/cleaned_data" / f"{args.model_name}.jsonl"
if cleaned.exists():
    print(f"[race] purging stale cleaned cache {cleaned.name} (forces re-clean of the raw target)")
    cleaned.unlink()
```
Mechanism being closed (all verified): `deepresearch_bench_race.py:250` scores the CLEANED file; `--force` (`score_report_race.py:86`) only bypasses the eval-results cache (`deepresearch_bench_race.py:365`), never the clean cache; `utils/clean_article.py:366-375` dedups by task-id only (`:369 to_process` filter, `:373-375` early return; worker-side skip at `:273`; id loader `:427-449`) — so a stale/aborted 1090-char stub with id=72 permanently shadows every future real report under the same model name. The wrapper writes exactly one raw record, so purging the whole per-model cleaned file is exact.
**(b) `scripts/score_report_race.py` : `main` : after the harness `subprocess.run` (~:89)** — scored-artifact assertion: read the cleaned file, assert one record with `id == task["id"]` and `len(article) >= 0.5 * len(report_text)` (LLM cleaning shrinks slightly, never 30x); on failure print `BLOCKED: cleaned/raw divergence` and return 3. This also catches the `{"id","error"}` failure record `clean_article.py:297` can leave (which `_load_processed_ids:440` counts as processed — a second poisoning vector).
**(c) Optional hardening, `scripts/run_gate_e2e.py:_race_command` (~:418):** the hardcoded stable `--model-name polaris_gate_task{task_id}` is what let the poison persist across runs; suffix with the draw run-id (`polaris_gate_task72_{run_id[-6:]}`) so score namespaces never collide. (a)+(b) already make this safe; (c) is belt-and-suspenders.
**Faithfulness:** eval tooling only; zero pipeline contact.
**RACE impact:** none on the true score; eliminates the 0.0384-class catastrophic false reading forever.

### FIX 3 — Remove the D8 banner drag by making D8 actually run (never by hiding it)
**(a) `scripts/run_gate_e2e.py` : `_fresh_e2e_env_slate` (:317-333)** — add `"PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE": "1"` on live runs.

### FIX 4 — Report shape: ship a literature review, not an audit dump (the direct IF fix)
Diagnosis of why IF is WORSE (0.3682 vs champion 0.4587) even after cache purge — four verified mechanisms:
1. **No H1 title, no introduction** (report opens on a blockquote; champion opens `# A literature review on…` + `## Introduction and Scope`).
2. **Structure inversion:** 14 of 20 headed blocks are machinery. Verified render sites, all sweep-side (NOT frozen): Contradiction disposition ledger `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:4932-4940`; Contradictions (both sides) `:5038`; Reliability header `:6273`; appendix boundary constant `:6325`; Evidence-support disclosure `:17130` (block body built by frozen `provenance_generator.py:3124-3159` but *placed* by the sweep); Methods — source hygiene `:17673`; Single-source findings `src/polaris_graph/generator/key_findings.py:1064`; the Key-Findings hedge preamble `key_findings.py:~1004-1017`.
3. **The instruction (“only cites high-quality … journal articles”) is visibly violated AND visibly unevaluated** (bibliography 45% journal; audit all-UNKNOWN) — fixed by Fix 1 + Fix 5.
4. **Malformed rendering:** report line 9 begins `Job Displacement by AI Technologies.** …` — the bullet lost its opening `**Job Creation vs. ` fragment. The section title exists intact at line 24, so a mid-line chop occurred at render; prime suspect is the render-seam chrome/truncated-unit removal (log `:3258` `[render-seam] removed 2 chrome/truncated unit(s)`), which must never remove a substring inside a Key-Findings bullet.

Changes (all in non-frozen render assembly):
**(a) Title + intro.** At the sweep's report assembly (the seam that currently emits `## Key Findings` first): emit `# {title}` (the run title already exists — the abort path writes one at `:12922-12923`) followed by a short, claim-free `## Introduction and Scope` framing paragraph built from the contract objective (the champion's intro is exactly this kind of meta-prose and passed at 0.4447). Faithfulness-safe: framing asserts no findings; contains no citations; identical class of text to the champion's shipped intro.
**(b) Demote machinery below the appendix boundary.** Move the renders from sites listed in (2) so on-disk order is: Title → Intro → thematic `###` sections → Synthesis → Limitations → Bibliography → `## Appendix: audit, disclosure, and weighting (not scored as report claims)` (`:6325`) containing Methods-telemetry, Evidence-support disclosure, Single-source findings, both contradiction ledgers, Reliability header, source hygiene. Nothing is deleted — position only. Per CLAUDE.md §-1.3 placement/repetition is QUALITY, not faithfulness (precedent: the existing render-only dedup at `:20802-20809` runs after all verdicts are final on the same argument). The disclosure remains in the shipped file AND manifest.
**(c) Key-Findings integrity invariant.** In `key_findings.py` bullet emission (and/or a post-assembly check next to the citation normalizer): every bullet must start with `**` and contain a matched closing `**`; a bullet that fails is re-emitted from its stored title + carried sentence (both exist verbatim upstream), never shipped chopped. Also constrain the render-seam chrome removal to whole-unit granularity within the Key Findings block.
**(d) Shrink the hedge preamble** (report line 7) to one sentence with a pointer to the appendix (site: `key_findings.py` preamble emit ~:1004-1017).
**Faithfulness:** every moved block is disclosure/audit text; every kept finding sentence is byte-identical strict_verify output; the frozen files are 0-diff. Bullet re-emission uses only verbatim verified text already in the section results.
**RACE impact:** this is the mechanism the evidence pack's §5 identified as the collapse driver. Estimate +0.03-0.06 IF (deliverable now *is* a literature review), +0.04-0.07 Readability (title/intro/flow, no fragment bullets), +0.01-0.03 Comprehensiveness (judge attention no longer spent on machinery).

### FIX 5 — Tame the (about-to-arm) eligibility mask + fix the journal-only leak, without touching strict_verify
Once Fix 1 lands, `build_quality_eligibility` arms with a corpus that is 41% UNKNOWN tier (log `:2578`) — the HARD branch (`quality_eligibility.py:274-281`) would mask FAIL+UNKNOWN ≈ 48% of the menu, worse than this run. Pair these:
**(a) Resolve UNKNOWN instead of masking it — enable LLM tiering.** `PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_TIERING=1` (`tier_classifier.py:1368-1378`; it was OFF — zero tiering lines in the log). The 41% UNKNOWN collapses to real tiers before the mask reads them. Metadata enrichment, upstream, faithfulness-neutral.
**(b) Deterministic second-chance before UNKNOWN.** `quality_eligibility.py:score_source_quality` between steps 4 and 5 (insert before line 217): if the row carries a DOI or classifies as a journal article (`document_type_classifier.is_peer_reviewed_journal_article` :448 — the exact predicate `scope_facet_classifier.py:223-234` already trusts), return PASS with basis `doi/journal-genre evidence (tier absent)`. Evidence-POSITIVE only: FAIL verdicts (retracted `:193`, predatory `:198`, `is_peer_reviewed=False` `:207`, low tier `:214`) are untouched — the gate is never relaxed, only better informed. Also extend `_PREDATORY_HOST_PATTERNS` (`:142-150`) with `abacademies.org` (bib entry [9], the predatory-adjacent venue that shipped).
**(c) Two-tier topicality (this run's ACTUAL over-mask).** The 50 exclusions were cosine < 0.30 on the short `statement+direct_quote` surface (`quality_eligibility.py:121-125`), with the floor inherited from `PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR` (call site `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:14369`). Roughly half the exclusion band sits at 0.22-0.30 — on-topic-adjacent content lost. Change at the call site (`:14371-14379`): introduce `PG_TOPICALITY_HARD_FLOOR` (default `0.5 × floor`, i.e. 0.15) for the HARD quarantine, and pass the 0.15-0.30 band through the existing SOFT demote path (`quality_eligibility.py:350-351`, already implemented — `is_hard=False` per-row is a 3-line split of the loop). Confirmed off-topic junk (the SEC-10K/agri-food class, cosines 0.00-0.13 in the receipts) still hard-quarantines.
**(d) Journal-only: enforce as visible preference + measured compliance, not a starvation mask.** With Fix 1, `allowed_source_kinds=['peer_reviewed_journal']` (hard) reaches the policy but only becomes an `op=prefer` facet (`retrieval_projection.py:314-316`). Recommended enforcement stack, in order of safety:
   1. **Selection ordering:** journal rows (T1/T2 or DOI-verified per (b)) first in the selected menu (evidence_selector tier-balanced strategy already orders; add the journal key), so composition draws journals preferentially — bibliography share rises without dropping anything.
   2. **S4 audit made real:** with the true contract, `contract_compliance` can now score `scope.source_types` from the bibliography (journal share) instead of UNKNOWN — and the Methods block can truthfully state the enforcement.
   3. **Hard journal-only** (`journal_only_filter.py`, `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY=1`, fail-closed to T1/T2 at :52) only behind a corpus-adequacy pre-check (≥ ~25 T1/T2/DOI rows post-tiering); otherwise disclose "insufficient journal corpus; prioritized journals" rather than starve. Empirical justification: the champion scored IF 0.4587 while itself citing majority non-journal — the RACE judge rewards a coherent review with visible journal weighting far more than it rewards a starved-but-pure one; a hard mask on THIS corpus (9 journal bib entries) would crater Comprehensiveness.
**Faithfulness:** all changes shape the citable MENU upstream of the frozen verifier; no FAIL row is ever re-admitted; UNKNOWN handling changes add evidence, not leniency; `provenance_generator.py`/strict_verify remain 0-diff.
**RACE impact:** +0.02-0.04 Comprehensiveness/Insight (menu back from 93 → ~120-135 of 143; more corroboration candidates for the depth layer, which this run had at cross=0), +0.01-0.03 IF (visible journal compliance + audit SATISFIED rows).
